name: Save to Pinecone
description: This process involves storing document embeddings in a Pinecone vector database for efficient similarity search and retrieval.
input: It may take in Dataframe as an input

fields.name: contentCol
fields.title: Select Content Column
fields.description: Column containing the original text or document content associated with each embedding. Used for traceability, debugging, and downstream retrieval workflows.

fields.name: embeddingsCol
fields.title: Select Embeddings Column
fields.description: Column containing vector embeddings to be stored in Pinecone. Each row must contain a numeric vector matching the configured index dimension.

fields.name: pineconeConnection
fields.title: Select Pinecone Connection
fields.description: Select the configured Pinecone connection that provides authentication, environment, and access details for writing vectors to the database.

fields.name: pineconeIndexName
fields.title: Index Name
fields.description: Name of the Pinecone index where embeddings will be stored. If the index does not exist, it may be created based on the provided configuration.

fields.name: indexNameSpace
fields.title: Index Namespace
fields.description: Logical namespace within the Pinecone index used to segment and organize vectors. Useful for separating datasets, versions, or tenants.

fields.name: dimension
fields.title: Dimension
fields.description: Dimensionality of the embedding vectors. Must exactly match the embedding model used and the target Pinecone index configuration.

fields.name: metric
fields.title: Metric
fields.description: Similarity metric used by the Pinecone index to compare vectors during search and retrieval operations.

fields.name: metadataCols
fields.title: Metadata Columns
fields.description: Optional columns to store as metadata alongside each vector. Enables filtered search, attribution, and richer retrieval results.

fields.name: uniqueIdCols
fields.title: Unique ID Columns
fields.description: Columns used to construct a stable and unique identifier for each vector. Helps prevent duplicates and enables deterministic upserts.
